headline: Colgate Palmolive Beat Highlights Emerging Markets Strength And AI Productivity Focus
summary: Colgate Palmolive (NYSE:CL) reported fourth quarter 2025 results that exceeded expectations on earnings and revenue. The company highlighted solid organic growth in emerging markets and resilience during a period of macroeconomic challenges. Management emphasized a focus on artificial intelligence and productivity efforts as key priorities for the business. Colgate Palmolive, known for its oral care, personal care and home care brands, sits at the center of everyday consumer spending. For...

headline: Kuwait confirms reports of oil production cuts
summary: Investing.com — Kuwait has confirmed reports that it reduced oil and refinery production on Saturday, citing disruptions linked to declining shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz.

raw.headline: Kuwait confirms reports of oil production cuts

headline: BofA flags large CTA selling in S&P 500, Nasdaq last week
summary: Investing.com — Commodity trading advisors (CTAs) carried out significant equity selling last week, particularly in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, as systematic funds rapidly reduced risk following recent market volatility, according to Bank of America Global Research.

headline: Oil at $100 could lift U.S. inflation, but persistence is key, Barclays says
summary: Investing.com — A surge in oil prices toward $100 per barrel would likely push U.S. headline inflation higher in the near term, although the longer-term impact will depend largely on how long prices remain elevated, according to analysts at Barclays.

raw.headline: Oil at $100 could lift U.S. inflation, but persistence is key, Barclays says
raw.summary: Investing.com — A surge in oil prices toward $100 per barrel would likely push U.S. headline inflation higher in the near term, although the longer-term impact will depend largely on how long prices remain elevated, according to analysts at Barclays.

headline: Fed’s Hammack warns of potential ‘tighter’ policy as oil shock lingers
summary: Investing.com — Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack signaled on Friday that the U.S. central bank may be forced to weigh tighter monetary policy later this year if inflation progress stalls, even as a cooling labor market adds a new layer of complexity to the Fed’s dual mandate.
